# AnswerLoops

> AnswerLoops turns a stream of community support questions (Discord, Slack, GitHub, Telegram, email, website chat) into a self-improving knowledge base that deflects repeat questions automatically, escalating only the long tail to a human.

## What it does

A community member asks a question. AnswerLoops classifies it, searches the org's knowledge base and past resolved tickets for a matching answer, drafts a grounded response, and grades that response's confidence. High-confidence answers post automatically. Low-confidence answers route to a human support queue with an AI-drafted reply ready to edit. Every resolved answer feeds back into the knowledge base, so the same question gets cheaper and faster to answer next time.

## Who it's for

Open-source maintainers, developer-tool companies, and community teams who get repeat support questions across Discord, Slack, GitHub Issues/Discussions, Telegram, email, or their own website — and want an AI layer that answers correctly instead of hallucinating, with humans only touching the questions the AI isn't sure about.

## Core capabilities

- **Confidence-gated auto-answer** — a second AI pass grades every drafted answer before it goes out; only answers that clear the bar post automatically, everything else queues for human review
- **Multi-channel ingest** — Discord, Slack, GitHub Issues/Discussions, Telegram, email, and an embeddable website chat widget, all through one pipeline
- **Self-improving knowledge base** — resolved tickets promote into a searchable KB; community 👍/👎 feedback prunes bad answers over time
- **Bring your own LLM** — OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Groq, Mistral, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (including local models via Ollama); each org configures its own key and cost
- **Agent-first API access** — an MCP server (`POST /api/mcp`, JSON-RPC 2.0) lets AI agents (Claude, Cursor, custom bots) call `search_kb`, `get_faq`, `get_tickets`, `create_ticket`, and `generate_answer` directly against an org's data, authenticated with a per-org API key
- **Self-hostable** — AGPL-3.0 licensed; `docker compose up` runs the full stack on your own infrastructure with no data leaving your servers
- **Analytics** — deflection rate, hours saved, per-category accuracy, SLA attainment, all computed from real ticket data

## Pricing

Self-hosted: free forever (AGPL-3.0). Hosted (managed SaaS): Pro $29/mo, Scale $79/mo, Enterprise $299/mo — all with a 14-day free trial, tiered by monthly deflection volume.
